message: 🌱 Add cilium: k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts mission (#364)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cilium-19038-k8sservicehost-multiple-ips-hosts",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cilium: k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts",
+    "description": "k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts. This issue affects 36+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify cilium feature symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=cilium\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors related to: k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Node configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cilium resources:\n```bash\nkubectl get node -A\nkubectl describe node <name> -n <namespace>\n```\n## Proposal / RFE\n\n**Is your feature request related to a problem?**\nno, it's just a inconvenience on bare-metal\n\n**Describe the solution you'd like**\ncurrently we run multiple clusters with cilium an"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts",
+        "description": "See commit message for detailed description\n\nIntroduce a new command line parameter(`--k8s-api-server-urls`) and helm option(`k8s.apiServerURLs`) to specify multiple k8s API server addresses for the client to use.\n\nFixes: #19038\n```yaml\nthis would make it much more easy to use the kube-proxy less mode when running on bare-metal since an external load balancer would not be needed, especially when running with something like k3s where the master's do load balancer internally.\r\n\r\n(btw. at the moment we use kube-vip for that, but preferably we would drop it, once k8s is up and running you can use metallb and announce the kube-api masters by using a endpoint and svc and feed that to metallb)\r\n\nSee commit message for detailed description\r\n\r\nIntroduce a new command line parameter(`--k8s-api-server-urls`) and helm option(`k8s.apiServ\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm k8sServiceHost multiple IPs/Hosts is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The root cause is: See commit message for detailed description\n\nIntroduce a new command line parameter(`--k8s-api-server-urls`) and helm option(`k8s.apiServerURLs`) to specify multiple k8s API server addresses for the client to use.\n\nFixes: #19038. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "this would make it much more easy to use the kube-proxy less mode when running on bare-metal since an external load balancer would not be needed, especially when running with something like k3s where the master's do load balancer internally.\r\n\r\n(btw. at the moment we use kube-vip for that, but preferably we would drop it, once k8s is up and running you can use metallb and announce the kube-api masters by using a endpoint and svc and feed that to metallb)\r\n\nSee commit message for detailed description\r\n\r\nIntroduce a new command line parameter(`--k8s-api-server-urls`) and helm option(`k8s.apiServerURLs`) to specify multiple k8s API server addresses for the client to use.\r\n\r\nFixes: #19038",
+        "@fristonio is this something you're still working on?\nare there any updates regarding this issue? for clusters which do not have an external apiserver load balancer having to specify one ip address is quite inconvenient.\n> are there any updates regarding this issue? for clusters which do not have an external apiserver load balancer having to specify one ip address is quite inconvenient.\r\n\r\ncilium with cube-proxy replacement is unusable now for high available prod deployments. Need ability to set several API Server addresses.\r\n\r\nDoes anybody plan to fix this issue?\n> > are there any updates regarding this issue? for clusters which do not have an external apiserver load balancer having to specify one ip address is quite inconvenient.\r\n> \r\n> cilium with cube-proxy replacement is unusable now ",
+        "so at least k3s users can use this instead of waiting that it might be done at some point\n@bittermandel That would be great! I think a good approach would be to revive https://github.com/cilium/cilium/pull/20090, and then update the list of kube-apiserver URLs during runtime once a connectivity has been established to any of them.\n> @bittermandel That would be great! I think a good approach would be to revive #20090, and then update the list of kube-apiserver URLs during runtime once a connectivity has been established to any of them.\r\n\r\nI'll check it out :+1:\r\n\r\nWith updating it in runtime, do you mean replacing the static list of apiservers in the Operator DaemonSet with a list of ips taken from listing the `Node` objects? Or reconfiguring the client config during runtime for that pod on"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
